title: Podcast Transcript
description: Schema for realistic multi-speaker podcast transcripts intended for text-to-speech rendering. Overlapping speech and reactions are anchored to specific words or phrases in other utterances via the anchor field.

hosts.description: All speakers who appear in this transcript.

utterances.description: Ordered list of all speech acts and reactions. A single speaker may produce multiple consecutive utterances. Order reflects narrative sequence, not strict speaker alternation.

id.description: Short identifier used in utterances[].speaker. Lowercase, no spaces.

voices.description: Optional TTS voice references for this speaker, one per provider. Looked up by provider name at render time.

provider.description: The TTS provider this voice_id belongs to.

speaker.description: References hosts[].id. The person producing this audio.

type.description: Classification of the utterance. Affects how the renderer treats timing and audio.
enumDescriptions: Normal speech. The speaker may hold the floor for as long as needed across one or more consecutive utterances.
Speaker cuts in or talks over another speaker.
Brief affirmation while another speaker holds the floor: 'mm-hmm', 'yeah', 'right'.
Non-verbal or near-verbal reaction: laughter, gasp, sigh. text is often empty.

text.description: The words spoken. Empty string for pure non-verbal reactions. No SSML or markup — keep this clean so it can be fed to any TTS backend.

anchor.description: Optional. For interjections, backchannels, and reactions: identifies the exact moment in another utterance that triggered this one. Used by editors and mixers to sync placement.

reaction.description: Required when type is 'reaction'. Describes the non-verbal event.

delivery.description: Speech characterisation cues for the TTS renderer or voice director.

emotion.description: Dominant emotional register for this turn. Used to select TTS style or guide a voice director.

pace.description: Speaking speed relative to the host's natural baseline.

volume.description: Loudness relative to the host's natural baseline.

emphasis.description: Words or short phrases within text that should receive strong stress. Must appear verbatim in text.

notes.description: Free-form instruction for nuance that structured fields cannot express. Written for a voice director or a capable TTS system prompt.
examples: Stresses 'literally' twice for comedic effect, slight pause before 'rewiring'.
Sharp intake of breath before the word, clearly cutting in.

Anchor.description: Pins this utterance to a specific word or phrase in another utterance. utterance_id must match an existing Utterance.id; phrase must appear verbatim in that utterance's text.

utterance_id.description: The id of the utterance this reaction or interjection is responding to.

phrase.description: The word or short phrase within the referenced utterance that triggered this response. Must appear verbatim in the referenced utterance's text. Used as the sync point for mixing.

kind.description: The type of non-verbal or near-verbal sound.

utterances.speaker: alex
utterances.text: So the study found that people who sleep fewer than six hours are literally — and I mean literally — rewiring their brain.

delivery.notes: Stresses 'literally' twice for comedic effect. Slight pause before 'rewiring'.
Sharp inhale just before the word. Clearly cutting in.
Brief self-aware chuckle — knows the claim sounds wild.

utterances.speaker: jamie
utterances.text: Okay, no, hold on. What does 'rewiring' even mean here? Are we talking metaphor or are they actually seeing this on scans?

delivery.notes: Leans on 'rewiring' mockingly. Genuine curiosity takes over for the second question.
